region_of_origin: North America (popularised in vegan cuisine 1980s–1990s); widely prepared in Singapore vegan cafés and homes
food_subgroup: Plant-based protein dish; composite vegan breakfast; silken tofu-based scramble with nutritional yeast, bell peppers, onion, and turmeric

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for tofu scramble as a composite dish. Closest parents are FOODON_03315660 (tofu) and a generic stir-fried vegetable dish node. Null assigned pending FoodOn composite dish expansion.
ontology.foodex2_description: Tofu and soy-based products — closest FoodEx2 anchor for silken tofu-based composite dish; used as analogous parent code
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A038T covers tofu and soy-based products. No specific composite tofu scramble code exists. A038T used as the closest defensible parent anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for tofu scramble as a prepared composite dish. Null assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 200g of cooked tofu scramble, equivalent to a breakfast plate portion prepared from 120g silken tofu, 30g bell peppers, 20g onion, 10g nutritional yeast, and spices with cooking oil.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Silken Tofu (SR Legacy) + USDA Branded Foods (Nutritional Yeast: Bragg / Red Star T6635+); composite recipe calculation per 100g cooked weight
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Branded Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are calculated from a composite recipe: silken tofu (USDA SR Legacy), fortified nutritional yeast (USDA Branded Foods — Bragg Nutritional Yeast and Red Star T6635+), bell peppers (USDA SR Legacy), onion (USDA SR Legacy), and cooking oil (USDA SR Legacy). Values are expressed per 100g of the cooked composite dish. B12 and B-vitamin values apply only when fortified nutritional yeast is used; unfortified nutritional yeast provides negligible B12.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Soy-Based Foods and Tofu — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate for silken tofu composite dish
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~15 is an analogous estimate based on published GI values for silken and soft tofu in the University of Sydney GI Database (soy-based foods, GI range 15–20). The composite dish has very low carbohydrate content (4g/100g; 8g per 200g serving), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 1.2 per 200g serving. Bell peppers and onion contribute minimal additional carbohydrate. The GI estimate is conservative and consistent with peer-reviewed literature on soy protein foods and low-GI breakfast patterns.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 200mg per 100g (400mg per 200g serving) from added salt during cooking
hypertension.guidance: Tofu scramble contains 400mg sodium per standard 200g serving from added salt; individuals with hypertension should reduce added salt to achieve approximately 100mg sodium per 100g, and the potassium content (370mg per 200g serving from bell peppers and onion) supports a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio for blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index (~15) and glycemic load (~1.2 per 200g serving); high soy protein content with insulin-sensitising properties
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tofu scramble has a GI of approximately 15 and a glycemic load of only 1.2 per 200g serving, making it one of the lowest-GI complete breakfast options available; the 11.5g soy protein per serving supports postprandial satiety and has documented insulin-sensitising effects, making this dish highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero cholesterol, low saturated fat (1.4g per 200g serving), soy isoflavones, and anti-inflammatory curcumin from turmeric
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Tofu scramble contains zero cholesterol and only 1.4g saturated fat per 200g serving; soy protein has been associated with modest LDL-cholesterol reduction in meta-analyses, and the curcumin from turmeric (bioavailability enhanced by black pepper piperine in this recipe) provides additional anti-inflammatory benefit relevant to cardiovascular risk reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High folate (240mcg per 200g serving) and B12 (2.4mcg per 200g serving) from fortified nutritional yeast; curcumin anti-inflammatory activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For RA patients on methotrexate, the 240mcg folate per 200g serving (60% of daily RDA) from fortified nutritional yeast is clinically significant for mitigating methotrexate-induced folate depletion; B12 (2.4mcg per serving) is also relevant, and curcumin bioavailability is enhanced by the black pepper in this recipe — note that B12 and folate values apply only when fortified nutritional yeast is used.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tofu scramble is a vegan breakfast dish that transforms silken tofu into a scrambled egg-like preparation through pan-frying with turmeric (which imparts a yellow colour and provides curcumin), cumin, black pepper, bell peppers, and onion. When prepared with fortified nutritional yeast, it becomes one of the most nutritionally complete vegan breakfasts available. Per 200g serving: approximately 168 kcal, 11.5g protein, 9.4g fat, 8g carbohydrate, GI ~15, glycemic load ~1.2, 2.4mcg Vitamin B12, and 240mcg folate — the latter two derived entirely from fortified nutritional yeast. The turmeric and black pepper combination is nutritionally intentional: piperine in black pepper enhances curcumin bioavailability by up to 2,000%, and the fat from cooking oil and tofu facilitates absorption of fat-soluble curcumin. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, this is among the lowest-GI complete breakfast options available. For RA patients on methotrexate, the folate content alone (240mcg per serving) is clinically therapeutic. CRITICAL NOTE: B12 and elevated B-vitamin values in this dish come ONLY from FORTIFIED nutritional yeast. Unfortified nutritional yeast provides B vitamins but negligible B12. Consumers should check labels for 'Vitamin B12 added' or 'cyanocobalamin fortified'. Bragg Nutritional Yeast and Red Star T6635+ are reliably fortified brands available in Singapore at Ryan's Grocery, Supernature, Scoop Wholefoods, and NTUC FairPrice. The dish is vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal. Texture is soft and crumbly, resembling scrambled eggs. Taste is savoury and umami-rich from the nutritional yeast, with earthy warmth from turmeric and cumin.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from a composite recipe calculation using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (silken tofu, bell peppers, onion) and USDA Branded Foods (fortified nutritional yeast — Bragg and Red Star T6635+). No single approved-source entry exists for the exact composite dish. Values are well-supported for the individual components but carry inherent uncertainty from recipe proportions and cooking-loss assumptions. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database soy-food data. Confidence set to medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for the composite tofu scramble dish is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB database; individual component fatty acid data could be summed but is not reported here to avoid compounding estimation error. water_g is set to -1: moisture content of the cooked composite dish is not directly reported in the source database entries used. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: silken tofu and the other ingredients in this recipe contain negligible vitamin D; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for silken tofu and bell peppers. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: all ingredients are plant-derived; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. trans_fat_g is 0.0: no hydrogenated fats used; confirmed negligible per USDA FoodData Central. 3. B12 and folate values (vitamin_b12_mcg 1.2 per 100g; folate_mcg_dfe 120 per 100g) apply ONLY when fortified nutritional yeast is used. Unfortified nutritional yeast provides negligible B12. Consumers must verify fortification status on product labels. 4. Sodium 200mg/100g assumes approximately 0.5g added salt per 200g serving during cooking; low-sodium preparation reduces this to approximately 100mg/100g. 5. GI ~15 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database soy-food entries; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish exists in the approved GI database. 6. FoodEx2 code A038T used as closest defensible parent anchor for tofu and soy-based products; no specific composite tofu scramble code exists in FoodEx2.

improvement_actions_taken: Restructured input JSON into RESULT template format.
Added Tamil (டோஃபு வறுவல்) and Hindi (टोफू स्क्रैम्बல) local names and aliases to satisfy G6 multilingual compliance for Singapore-context dish.
Expanded aliases_by_language with all language keys and verified consistency with flat aliases array.

Replaced input health_suitability array with structured health_context object including Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes (human_condition_name corrected), and Cardiovascular Disease with concern_level, key_factor, and clinical guidance.
Added FoodEx2 A038T as defensible ontology anchor with gap note; retained FoodOn and SNOMED CT as null with honest gap notes.
Documented all -1 fields (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g) and all 0.0 fields (vitamin_d_mcg, cholesterol_mg, trans_fat_g) in notes per G3 requirements.
Expanded llm_training.natural_language_description to exceed 200 characters with full clinical, cultural, and nutritional context.
Set confidence to 'medium' with justification reflecting composite recipe estimation basis.
Verified GI field is integer 15 (not null, not zero) appropriate for a carbohydrate-containing food.
